title: Crataegus × canescens
taxonomy: Crataegus × canescens is classified as a hybrid species within the genus Crataegus, which belongs to the family Rosaceae. It is a cross between two different species of hawthorn, Crataegus monogyna and Crataegus laevigata. The '×' symbol in its name indicates that it is a hybrid. The classification of Crataegus × canescens reflects its unique genetic makeup and distinct characteristics. This hybrid hawthorn is often cultivated for its ornamental value and attractive flowers.
uses: Crataegus × canescens, also known as the gray hawthorn, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves, bark, and fruits contain compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties make it potentially useful in treating conditions such as high blood pressure and digestive issues. In addition, the plant's extracts have been studied for their antimicrobial and antiviral activities. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal applications of Crataegus × canescens.
benefits: Crataegus × canescens, also known as Washington hawthorn, has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits. The plant is said to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular properties. Some studies suggest that it may help to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as reduce the risk of heart disease. Additionally, the extracts from Washington hawthorn have been reported to have antimicrobial and antiviral properties. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Washington hawthorn for various health purposes.
properties: Crataegus × canescens is a hawthorn species with potential therapeutic properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. It is believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect against cardiovascular disease and other conditions. Studies suggest that Crataegus × canescens may also have immunomodulatory and neuroprotective properties. The plant's flavonoids and polyphenols are thought to contribute to its potential therapeutic benefits. Further studies are required to fully understand the therapeutic properties of Crataegus × canescens and its potential uses in medicine.
constituents: Crataegus × canescens is known to contain a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids. These active constituents are believed to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. The flavonoids present in Crataegus × canescens are thought to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The phenolic acids may also contribute to the plant's antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Crataegus × canescens.
parts: Crataegus × canescens, commonly known as Washington hawthorn, is a shrub or small tree species. The medicinal parts of Crataegus × canescens primarily involve the fruits, leaves, and flowers. The fruits, also known as hawthorn berries, are often used in traditional medicine to support cardiovascular health. The leaves and flowers of the plant are also used in herbal remedies, often in combination with the berries. In traditional Chinese medicine, the berries of Crataegus × canescens are valued for their potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve heart function.
preparations: Crataegus × canescens is known for its traditional use in herbal medicine. The plant's leaves and fruits are used to make various preparations. The berries of Crataegus × canescens contain compounds that may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations made from the plant are believed to have potential benefits for cardiovascular health and immune system function. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these preparations.
side_effects: Crataegus × canescens, also known as the Washington hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. However, consuming Crataegus × canescens may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. The fruit, leaves, and flowers of the plant contain flavonoids and other compounds that may have a blood-thinning effect, which can increase the risk of bleeding. Additionally, people with kidney or liver disease, as well as those taking certain medications, should use caution when consuming Crataegus × canescens due to its potential interactions. More research is needed to fully understand the side effects and potential interactions of Crataegus × canescens.
intro: Crataegus × canescens, also known as the hybrid hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to help lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Crataegus × canescens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, such as oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) and flavonols, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include extracts, capsules, and tinctures, often used to support heart health and reduce symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
